headline: Vikings: Exploration and Culture
description: Vikings (Norse: víkingr = raider/pirate) were Scandinavian seafarers (8th-11th centuries) who explored, traded, and raided across Europe, reaching North America ~1000 CE — 500 years before Columbus. Beyond the raiding stereotype: skilled traders, craftsmen, shipbuilders, and storytellers. Runestones and sagas preserve their history.
